description:
Manual games allow you to set custom check locations and custom item names that will be rolled into a multiworld.
This allows any variety of game -- PC, console, board games, Microsoft Word memes... really anything -- to be part of a multiworld randomizer.
The key component to including these games is some level of manual restriction. Since the items are not actually withheld from the player,
the player must manually refrain from using these gathered items until the tracker shows that they have been acquired or sent.

1.0.2a.description:
# Changelog
- The Pink Stache wasn't labeled as Progression even though it's part of a check. This has been fixed.
1.0.2a.title: Version 1.0.2a

1.1.0.description:
# Changelog
- **Added goal option for collecting 50/58 (~86%) of patches throughout the multiworld.**
- Fixed Nostalgia Spikes' logic based on a more recent discovery of mine. See #1 to see what the intended method is.
- Added DeathLink.
1.1.0.title: Version 1.1.0

1.1.1.description:
# Changelog
- **The Manual is now compatible with 0.6.2+ of Archipelago.**
- Fixed a typo where `Pinecone Barrel Patch` was called `Pinecone Barrel`.
1.1.1.title: Version 1.1.1

1.1.1a.description:
# Changelog
- This Manual is now compatible with 0.6.3+ of Archipelago.
- Update: Added the Manual's files to GitHub for anyone who wishes to make PR's (Pull Requests) or overall assist with the Manual's development.
1.1.1a.title: Version 1.1.1a

1.2.0.description:
# Known Issues
None for now...
# Changelog
- **Updated the Manual to the latest Stable version.**
- **Added an Option for the Patch Macguffin Goal, which, instead of needed specifically 50 Patches, allows you to choose how many Patches you need to Goal your game.**
- **Made a map courtesy of [Wakamu's Mapping Editor Tool](https://github.com/Wakamu/archipelago-mapping-editor/releases/latest)!** 
  - To use it, simply [download the .apworld](https://github.com/Wakamu/Archipelago/releases/latest) and place it in your custom_worlds folder. After running the launcher, you should see a visual_packs folder. Place the zipped folder below in there and then run the Visual Tracker client in your launcher. Once you connect to your game, you'll be able to load the map pack and use it as a reference for where the game's various Checks can roughly be found!
1.2.0.title: Version 1.2.0

1.2.1.description:
# Known Issues
None for now...
# Changelog
## Manual
No changes.
## Map
- Made the map less terrible.
  - More specifically, it defaults to a set of 3 split images that allow you to see the map a lot better. You can still use the full map view if you wish, but I don't recommend it. The image's height makes it hard to make out anything on it well due to there being no ability to zoom.
1.2.1.title: Version 1.2.1
